title: Agent OS ROI review: change impact and ownership map
goal: Capture the ROI case for change-impact and ownership guidance from issue #1115.
Keep the review bounded to future-work discovery.
scope: Issue #1115 ROI point 2: change impact and ownership map.
Changed-path routing, ownership/authority mapping, generated-surface awareness, and likely proof surfaces.
non_goals: Do not build a dependency graph or ownership index in this review.

review_mode.review question: Would a changed-path impact map materially reduce wrong-owner edits and missed proof obligations?
review_mode.inputs inspected first: Issue #1115 body, implement changed-path classification, docs/agent-os-capabilities.md ownership and generated-surface sections

review_method.commands used: uv run agentic-workspace implement --changed <ten review artifact paths> --task "Create one planning review artifact for each of the ten agent OS capability ROI points from issue #1115" --format json

findings.title: Changed-path impact mapping is a high-ROI bridge between ownership and proof
findings.summary: The implement output already classifies changed paths and selects proof, but agents still need a richer explanation of owner module, related contracts, generated surfaces, docs, and likely tests for a proposed change.
findings.evidence: For these review artifacts, implement classified the change as planning-only, identified the exact planning paths, detected issue #1115, and selected planning summary plus planning doctor proof. Issue #1115 asks for the same path-to-owner and path-to-proof explanation as its second ROI item.
findings.risk if unchanged: Agents can make locally plausible edits while missing the real authority surface, generated-source dependency, or consumer proof that should have shaped the change.
findings.suggested action: Create a child slice that extends changed-path routing with an owner/impact explanation assembled from existing ownership, module, generated-surface, and proof metadata.
findings.promotion target: GitHub child issue under #1115; Workspace Composition plus Ownership / Authority Mapping
findings.promotion trigger: Promote when a change touches multiple owner surfaces, generated outputs, or a proof miss that an impact map could have predicted.
findings.post-remediation note shape: stub pointing to the impact-map child issue and the command output that owns the explanation

recommendation.promote: Second child slice after task contract, or pair with proof-confidence work if implementation surfaces overlap.
recommendation.defer: Keep inactive until the parent lane is selected.
recommendation.dismiss: Do not dismiss; ownership uncertainty is a recurring agent-cost source.

retention.trigger: A child issue or planning candidate captures impact-map scope.
retention.proof surface: Ownership command output, implement changed-path routing, and child slice validation.

validation_commands: uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
drift_log: 2026-05-24: Review record created from issue #1115 ROI point 2.
